Delta-method standard deviations of the natural parameters

Source: R/constructors.R
delta_method_sd.Rd

First-order (delta-method) standard deviations of a distribution's natural parameters, propagated from uncertain unnatural parameters. The uncertain unnatural parameters are treated as independent normals with standard deviations sds, and for each natural parameter the propagated standard deviation is sqrt(sum_i J[j, i]^2 * sds[i]^2), where the Jacobian J[j, i] = d(natural_j) / d(param_i) is estimated by central finite differences. Estimating the Jacobian numerically lets this work uniformly for every distribution type, including the Weibull, whose to_natural() solves for the shape numerically.

Usage

delta_method_sd(x, sds, natural, h_rel = 1e-04)

Arguments

x

A single <dist_spec> whose parameters are the unnatural parameters evaluated at their means.

sds

Numeric; the standard deviation of each unnatural parameter, in names(x$parameters) order (0 for a fixed parameter).

natural

The natural-parameter list evaluated at the means.

h_rel

Numeric; the relative step of the central finite difference. For parameter i the step is h_rel * max(abs(mean_i), 1), i.e. relative to the parameter value with an absolute floor near zero. The default 1e-4 keeps both the truncation error (order h^2) and the floating-point cancellation error (order eps / h) small for the smooth to_natural() maps. It is a numerical-differentiation constant exposed here for testing, deliberately kept off the user-facing constructors.

Value

A named numeric vector of standard deviations, one per natural parameter.

On this page

Developed by Sebastian Funk, James M. Azam, Sam Abbott.
